terms.definition: An immutable, ordered, fixed-size sequence in Python; commonly used as a lightweight record or hashable key.
Cannot be modified in place after creation; produces a new value if you want a change.
Implicit packing (`x = 1, 2`) and assignment-side unpacking (`a, b = pair`); `*rest` collects remaining items.
A factory in `collections` that builds a tuple subclass with named fields, combining tuple semantics with attribute access.
A typed, class-based form of namedtuple with type hints and methods.
A `@dataclass(frozen=True)` class — immutable, hashable record with named, typed fields and optional methods.
